title: Origin Fairness, Politeness, Retries, Backoff, and p-limit · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in publisher-respecting origin scheduler and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Maintain per-origin concurrency, minimum spacing, next-eligible time, request and byte budgets, and owner overrides.
Retry only classified transient and idempotent cases with attempt, elapsed, and owner budgets.
Parse valid Retry-After, combine it with capped exponential backoff and bounded jitter, and record the chosen delay.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Origin Buckets
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 8659 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove per-origin eligibility, retry classification, Retry-After, bounded jitter, p-limit boundaries, and clock control; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain per-origin concurrency, minimum spacing, next-eligible time, request and byte budgets, and owner overrides.

Inspect the Origin Buckets trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Clock Injection
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-clock-injection-142zye" that uses case 9118 to prove per-origin eligibility, retry classification, Retry-After, bounded jitter, p-limit boundaries, and clock control. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
